title: Unverified setSecurity allows arbitrary contract assignment
reason: `setSecurity()` is an `onlyMaster` governance action; this is a trust-model issue, not a permissionless protocol vulnerability.

title: Liquidation state inconsistency - token transfer after state update
reason: If `_safeTransferFrom()` fails, the EVM reverts the whole call and all earlier state changes are rolled back, so no persistent inconsistency is created.

title: Division by zero in fee share calculation
reason: With non-malicious fees at or below 100%, the denominator stays positive because `pseudoTotalPool` is increased before the division; reaching zero requires privileged fee misconfiguration.

title: Missing reentrancy protection in receive function
reason: `receive()` mutates no lending state and only forwards ETH with `transfer`; forced ETH acceptance is not an exploitable protocol bug here.

title: Missing zero address validation in approve function
reason: Approving the zero address is at most a harmless user mistake and does not create protocol-level harm.

title: No emergency stop mechanism
reason: This is a best-practice/governance observation rather than a concrete vulnerability in the current code.

title: Liquidation transaction can be delayed indefinitely
reason: Mempool delay is not controlled by contract code and no concrete exploit path is created by the implementation.

title: Collateral factor allows 100%
reason: The collateral factor is a governance-set risk parameter; allowing exactly 100% is a configuration choice, not a permissionless exploit.

title: Missing event emissions for critical functions
reason: Missing events affect observability only and do not directly endanger funds or protocol solvency.

title: Renounce ownership is permanent with no recovery
reason: Permanent ownership renunciation is expected behavior and a governance/trust-model decision, not a code vulnerability.

title: Fee manager NFT ID assumption
reason: This code intentionally hardcodes fee accounting to NFT id 0, and no evidence in the provided code shows that assumption is invalid.

title: Potential storage collision with isolated pools
reason: `positionLocked` is intentionally reused as the isolation-pool registration/lock bit; no unintended conflicting storage use was found.
